info.title: ThinClaw Gateway API
info.description: The v1 mobile contract of the ThinClaw web gateway: chat, threads, approvals, read-only jobs, gateway status, and device identity (pairing, device management, per-device tokens). Streaming events are delivered over `/api/chat/events` (SSE) or `/api/chat/ws` (WebSocket); both carry the `SseEvent` component schema.

202.description: Interrupt submitted to the agent loop

401.description: Missing or invalid gateway bearer token

400.description: Unknown approval action or malformed request id

401.description: Missing or invalid gateway bearer token

get.summary:
Authoritative pull surface for pending tool approvals: a mobile
client that was not holding an open SSE/WS stream when
`SseEvent::ApprovalNeeded` was broadcast can poll this endpoint instead of
missing the approval entirely. The registry is persisted across restarts
and drained only after an approval decision is accepted.

401.description: Missing or invalid gateway bearer token

get.description: Server-Sent Events stream of agent events. Each `data:` line is a JSON-encoded `SseEvent` discriminated by its `type` field. The stream has no replay: after a reconnect, clients must reconcile missed turns via `GET /api/chat/history`. Browser EventSource clients may pass the bearer token as a `token` query parameter; native clients must use the Authorization header.

401.description: Missing or invalid gateway bearer token

200.description: Turns for the requested (or active) thread

400.description: Malformed thread id or pagination cursor

401.description: Missing or invalid gateway bearer token

404.description: Thread not found or not visible to this identity

202.description: Message accepted for async processing; results stream over SSE/WS

401.description: Missing or invalid gateway bearer token

200.description: New side thread created and persisted

401.description: Missing or invalid gateway bearer token
Missing or invalid gateway bearer token

403.description: The pinned assistant thread cannot be deleted

404.description: Thread not found or not visible to this identity

200.description: Assistant thread plus regular conversation threads

401.description: Missing or invalid gateway bearer token

get.description: WebSocket upgrade for bidirectional chat. Client frames are JSON-encoded `WsClientMessage`; server frames are JSON-encoded `WsServerMessage` (SSE events in an envelope plus `pong`).

requestBody.description: Not a conventional request body: after the 101 upgrade, the client sends `WsClientMessage` frames.

401.description: Missing or invalid gateway bearer token
Missing or invalid gateway bearer token
Missing or invalid device token
Missing or invalid device token

post.description: The current device mints a reduced-scope companion (e.g. its paired watch): scopes are limited to chat + approvals (low-risk only, enforced server-side by device class). The companion is revoked whenever this parent is revoked (cascade). The raw token is returned exactly once.

200.description: Companion minted; token returned exactly once

401.description: Missing or invalid device token
Missing or invalid device token

404.description: Companion not found for this parent

401.description: Missing or invalid device token
Missing or invalid device token

put.description: Register a Live Activity update token for one activity. Include `thread_id` (agent runs) or `job_id` (jobs) so the gateway can route run-progress events to this activity's per-activity update token.

401.description: Missing or invalid device token
Missing or invalid device token
Missing or invalid device token
Missing or invalid device token

200.description: Device paired; token returned exactly once

401.description: Missing or invalid gateway bearer token
Missing or invalid gateway bearer token

200.description: Pairing approved; the device can now finalize with a repeat pair/complete call

401.description: Missing or invalid gateway bearer token

404.description: Pairing id not found or not awaiting confirmation

401.description: Missing or invalid gateway bearer token
Missing or invalid gateway bearer token

200.description: New token issued; returned exactly once

401.description: Missing or invalid gateway bearer token

200.description: Connection counts, uptime, cost/budget usage, runtime routing, and channel setup state

401.description: Missing or invalid gateway bearer token

get.description:
Returns `503` (so load balancers route away from a broken instance) unless
the database is reachable within 2s *and* at least one LLM provider is
configured. A deployment with no persistence configured (`store == None`) is
treated as DB-ready. The pure decision lives in
`thinclaw_gateway::web::status::readiness_response` (unit-tested there).

200.description: Gateway is ready (database reachable, LLM provider configured)

503.description: Gateway is degraded; load balancers should route away

200.description: Direct and sandbox jobs owned by the caller

401.description: Missing or invalid gateway bearer token
Missing or invalid gateway bearer token
Missing or invalid gateway bearer token

404.description: Job not found or not visible to this identity

thread_id.description: Thread that owns the pending approval (so the agent loop finds the right session).

ApprovalRisk.description:
Risk tier for a tool approval, computed gateway-side. Serialised
snake_case (`"low"` / `"high"`) on approval events and used to pick the push
category. Carried on [`crate::web::types::SseEvent::ApprovalNeeded`] and
[`crate::web::types::PendingApprovalEntry`].

CompanionListResponse.description:
`GET /api/devices/me/companions` response: the calling device's own
companions (never token/hash material).

CreateCompanionRequest.description:
`POST /api/devices/me/companions` request body: the current (parent) device
mints a reduced-scope companion (e.g. its paired watch). `platform`
defaults to `watchos` — the only companion surface in v1 — when omitted.

platform.description: Companion platform; defaults to `"watchos"` when absent or empty.

CreateCompanionResponse.description:
`POST /api/devices/me/companions` response — the only place the companion's
raw token is ever returned.

parent_device_id.description: The parent (minting) device id, echoed for the client's record.

token.description: Raw `tcd_...` companion token. Returned exactly once.

DeviceApnsRegistration.description:
Persisted APNs registration for a device's content-free pushes (D-N1).
Only the device token, target environment, and last-updated timestamp are
stored; payload content is never persisted here.

environment.description:
APNs environment: `"development"` (sandbox) or `"production"`. Selects
the APNs host when the pusher delivers to this device.

updated_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp of the last registration update.

DeviceInfo.description: Public view of a device (never includes token/hash material).

parent_device_id.description:
Parent device id when this is a companion device (milestone M4);
`None` for a normal top-level paired device.

DeviceLiveActivityKind.description:
The kind of Live Activity a registered push token drives (D-N2). Mirrors
the two activity surfaces the mobile app runs: an agent run and a
background job.

DeviceLiveActivityToken.description: Persisted Live Activity update-push token for one `activity_id` (D-N2).

job_id.description:
The background job this activity mirrors, when `kind == Job`. Lets the
notifier route job-completion events to this activity. `None` for agent
runs and legacy records.

kind.description: What the activity represents (agent run vs. job).

thread_id.description:
The chat thread this activity mirrors, when `kind == AgentRun`. Lets the
first-party notifier route run-progress events (keyed by `thread_id`) to
this activity's per-activity update token. `None` for job activities and
for legacy records written before the association existed.

updated_at.description:
RFC 3339 timestamp of the last registration update. Also the key used
for oldest-first eviction when the per-device cap is exceeded.

oneOf.description:
Anything else (e.g. future platforms, test fixtures). Carries the
raw platform string as reported at pairing time.

other.description:
Anything else (e.g. future platforms, test fixtures). Carries the
raw platform string as reported at pairing time.

DevicePlatform.description: Platform family of a paired device.

DeviceScope.description:
Device scopes (v1), per `docs/MOBILE_SECURITY.md` D-T4.

Never grantable to device tokens (enforced by never having a variant
here, and by [`super::scopes::required_scope`] returning `None` for
those routes): settings, secrets/providers, extensions/skills, memory
write, logs, restart, pairing admin.

has_more.description: Whether there are older messages available.

oldest_timestamp.description: Cursor for the next page (ISO8601 timestamp of the oldest message returned).

ModelInfo.description: Information about an available LLM model.

PairCompleteRequest.description:
`POST /api/devices/pair/complete` request body (public endpoint,
protected only by the one-time secret / human code).

pubkey.description: Base64 SPKI public key (D-P2). Stored, not yet enforced.

secret.description:
One-time 32-byte secret (base64url) from the QR, OR the short human
code — exactly one of the two redemption paths.

PairCompleteResponse.description:
`POST /api/devices/pair/complete` response — the only place the raw
device token is ever returned.

human_code.description:
Short human-typable code (no-camera fallback), same lockout as the
QR secret.

pairing_id.description:
Internal pairing-record id, used by the `require_confirm` admin
approve call.

qr_svg.description:
Self-contained SVG rendering of `qr_uri` for the authenticated gateway
pairing panel. Optional so newer clients remain compatible with older
gateways and non-visual gateway hosts can omit it.

PendingApprovalEntry.description:
One pending tool-approval request, as surfaced by `GET /api/chat/approvals`
(milestone B1 approvals-pull endpoint for mobile clients that are not
holding an open SSE/WS stream when the approval is raised).

Populated from the `StatusUpdate::ApprovalNeeded` -> `SseEvent::ApprovalNeeded`
projection at broadcast time (see `src/channels/web/mod.rs::send_status`),
and removed once the underlying decision is accepted. The gateway persists
this registry so reconnecting mobile clients receive an authoritative
snapshot across process restarts.

created_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp; entries are returned sorted oldest-first.

risk.description:
Gateway-computed risk tier (D-K3), matching
`SseEvent::ApprovalNeeded.risk`. Always present.

PendingPairInfo.description: A pending (not-yet-completed) pairing attempt, admin-facing view.

awaiting_confirm.description:
True once a `pair/complete` call has staged the device awaiting an
admin `approve` call (`require_confirm` mode).

QrPairingPayload.description:
QR payload embedded (base64url-json) in `thinclaw://pair?d=<...>`.

Field names are intentionally short — this travels through a QR code.
Unknown fields must be ignored by readers (versioned via `v`).

fp.description: base64url SHA-256 of the TLS leaf SPKI. Omitted only in `vpn-http` mode.

name.description: Human label for the gateway (shown in the pairing UI).

RegisterLiveActivityRequest.description:
`PUT /api/devices/me/live-activity/{activity_id}` request body: register
(or replace) the Live Activity update-push token for one activity.

job_id.description:
The background job this activity mirrors (for `kind == job`). Optional;
omit for agent-run activities.

kind.description: What the activity represents (agent run vs. job).

thread_id.description:
The chat thread this activity mirrors (for `kind == agent_run`). Lets
the gateway route run-progress events to this activity's update token
(D-N2). Optional; omit for job activities.

RegisterLiveActivityStartTokenRequest.description:
`PUT /api/devices/me/live-activity-start-token` request body: register (or
replace) the device's Live Activity push-to-start token.

RegisterPushRequest.description:
`PUT /api/devices/me/push` request body: register (or replace) the
device's APNs token for content-free pushes.

RotateTokenResponse.description:
`POST /api/devices/{id}/rotate` response — the only other place a raw
token is returned.

token.description: Newly issued raw `tcd_...` token. Returned exactly once.

client_message_id.description:
Optional client-generated UUID used to make offline/retry sends
idempotent across ambiguous transport failures.

oneOf.description:
Extended thinking / chain-of-thought reasoning from the LLM.
Sent alongside the Response event when extended thinking is enabled.

risk.description:
Gateway-computed risk tier (D-K3, single source of truth). Drives
the client's biometric gate and push category. Always present.

oneOf.description:
Agent requests a credential; the browser renders an inline masked-input
card that POSTs the value straight to `/api/repo-projects/credentials`.
Carries NO secret value — only the name to store under and a reason.
Extension activation status change (WASM channels).
Channel connectivity status change (channel came online/offline/degraded).
Routine lifecycle event (started, completed, failed).
Cost budget alert (approaching or exceeding daily/hourly limits).
Canvas / A2UI panel update pushed to the frontend.

Agent completed its bootstrap ritual (BOOTSTRAP.md deleted).
Frontend should update bootstrapNeeded → false.

assistant_thread.description:
The pinned assistant thread (always present after first load).

Schema note: emitted as a non-nullable, optional `$ref` to `ThreadInfo`
(`schema(nullable = false)` + `skip_serializing_if`), rather than the
default `oneOf: [null, $ref]` that utoipa produces for `Option<$ref>`.
swift-openapi-generator drops a `oneOf`-with-null property entirely, so
the null-union would make `assistant_thread` invisible to the generated
iOS client. The optional-ref shape keeps it as `ThreadInfo?`. On the wire
the field is now absent (not `null`) when `None`; the web UI already
coalesces via `data.assistant_thread || null`, so absent and `null` are
equivalent there.

ToolArtifact.description: Optional rich tool artifacts that can be rendered alongside the JSON result.

oneOf.description: Send a chat message to the agent.
Approve or deny a pending tool execution.

thread_id.description: Thread that owns the pending approval.

oneOf.description: Submit an auth token for an extension (bypasses message pipeline).
Cancel an in-progress auth flow.
Client heartbeat ping.
Protocol version handshake (sent on connect).
Set a configuration value on the orchestrator.
Set a secret on the orchestrator's keychain.
Request the list of available models.

WsClientMessage.description: Message sent by a WebSocket client to the server.

oneOf.description: An SSE-style event forwarded over WebSocket.

data.description: The event payload as a JSON value.

event_type.description: The event sub-type (response, thinking, tool_started, etc.)

oneOf.description: Server heartbeat pong.
Error message.
Protocol version info (response to client's Version message).

compatible.description: Whether the client version is compatible.

oneOf.description: Result of a config_set operation.
Result of a secret_set operation.
List of available models.

WsServerMessage.description: Message sent by the server to a WebSocket client.

gateway_token.description: Gateway bearer token (GATEWAY_AUTH_TOKEN or, once the device-identity layer ships, a per-device `tcd_` token).
